name: Rashida Tlaib
stanceSummary: Tlaib has repeatedly called for a ceasefire in Gaza, criticized Israeli military actions, and emphasized Palestinian human rights and self-determination.

timeline.event: Publicly called for a ceasefire in Gaza and de-escalation.
Spoke on House floor against broad military aid conditions without human rights constraints.

name: Cori Bush
stanceSummary: Bush has supported ceasefire resolutions and highlighted humanitarian impacts in Gaza.

timeline.event: Led and promoted ceasefire-focused messaging in Congress.
Reiterated opposition to unconditional military support in statements.

name: Ilhan Omar
stanceSummary: Omar has criticized Israeli government actions in Gaza and supported stronger human-rights based conditions on U.S. policy.

timeline.event: Called for ceasefire and expanded humanitarian access.
Advocated oversight of military aid and accountability for civilian harm.

name: Bernie Sanders
stanceSummary: Sanders has been critical of Israeli military conduct in Gaza while also affirming Israel's right to security, placing him in a mixed position in this framework.

timeline.event: Introduced efforts focused on oversight and conditions tied to military aid.
Repeated calls for humanitarian protections and policy recalibration.

name: Hakeem Jeffries
stanceSummary: Jeffries has strongly supported Israel and emphasized security cooperation while also discussing humanitarian concerns for civilians in Gaza.

timeline.event: Released statement affirming strong support for Israel following attacks.
Backed continued U.S.-Israel security assistance in leadership remarks.

name: Chuck Schumer
stanceSummary: Schumer has expressed strong alignment with Israel's security position while also calling for pathways to regional de-escalation.

timeline.event: Published statements supporting Israel and condemning Hamas attacks.
Delivered Senate floor remarks on Israeli government policy and long-term peace options.
